taxonomy: Malaxis myodes is classified within the family Orchidaceae, which is the largest and most diverse family of flowering plants. intro: Malaxis myodes, also known as the little green orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family (Orchidaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Bioactive constituents of Malaxis myodes include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Malaxis myodes is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the production of herbal teas and tinctures.
